name: NullEmailMessageRejected
concept: SaveEmailMessage() rejects null EmailMessage with a failure result before any DB operation
outcome: return Result.Fail<EmailMessage>("EmailMessage was null!") — no DB write performed

name: NullRecipientsRejected
concept: SendEmailMessages() requires non-null recipients collection; throws ArgumentNullException if null
outcome: ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(recipients) — operation aborts with exception

name: AttachmentFilterRule
concept: Attachments are filtered before processing: any attachment with null content, zero-length content, or blank filename is silently excluded
outcome: Attachments not meeting all three conditions are excluded from dbAttachments list; no error raised

name: SubjectMaxLength200
concept: Email subject is truncated to 200 characters before being stored in EmailMessage
outcome: subject.Truncate(200) applied — excess characters silently removed; no error raised

name: ReplyToMaxLength200
concept: ReplyTo field is truncated to 200 characters before being stored in EmailMessage
outcome: replyTo.Truncate(200) applied — excess characters silently removed; no error raised

name: RecipientEmailTrimmed
concept: Recipient email address is trimmed of leading and trailing whitespace before being stored
condition: recipient.Email has leading or trailing whitespace
outcome: recipient.Email.Trim() assigned to ToEmail — whitespace stripped silently

name: GatewayEmptyBodyOrSubjectRejected
concept: SendGridWorkloadProcessor rejects email dispatch if Body or Subject is empty; sets status ValidationError without sending
outcome: mail.Status = EmailStatus.ValidationError — SendGrid API is not called; message stranded at ValidationError

name: GatewayInvalidEmailAddressRejected
concept: SendGridWorkloadProcessor validates recipient email format before dispatch; invalid format sets status InvalidEmail
outcome: mail.Status = EmailStatus.InvalidEmail — SendGrid API is not called

name: InboundSenderMustBeWhitelisted
concept: InboundParseEventHandler silently drops inbound emails from senders not present in the Email2Sms whitelist
outcome: return Task.CompletedTask — no SMS sent, no error raised, debug logged

name: InboundRecipientMustBePhoneAddress
concept: Inbound email recipient address must match regex ^(\+?[0-9]+)@[^@]+$ to be processed as SMS; non-matching recipients are skipped with error log
outcome: log error and continue to next recipient — no SMS send attempted for this recipient

name: InboundNullEnvelopeDropped
concept: InboundParse endpoint drops email if envelope.To or From is null — publishes no event
outcome: return Ok() — no InboundParseEvent published, no SMS triggered

name: BroadcastReceiptNullGuards
concept: EmailSendBroadcastReceiptAsync() requires non-null profile, group, and sendToEmailsAndNames; throws on null
condition: profile == null || group == null || sendToEmailsAndNames == null
outcome: ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull() — operation aborts with exception for any null argument

name: TemplateBodyNullGuard
concept: Broadcast receipt email is only sent if generated emailBody is not null; null body results in silent no-send
outcome: SendEmailMessages() only called when emailBody has content; null body = no email sent, no error raised

name: InitialStatusImportingWhenAttachments
concept: EmailMessage is created with Status=Importing when attachments are present; transitions to Queued only after attachments are persisted
outcome: Status = EmailStatus.Importing at creation; Status updated to EmailStatus.Queued after InsertEmailAttachments + UpdateEmailMessages

name: InitialStatusQueuedWhenNoAttachments
concept: EmailMessage is created directly with Status=Queued when no attachments are present
outcome: Status = EmailStatus.Queued at creation — no intermediate Importing state

name: GatewaySuccessStatus
concept: On successful SendGrid API response (HTTP < 400): status transitions to SentToGateway (live) or SentToGatewaySandbox (test mode)
outcome: mail.Status = testMode ? EmailStatus.SentToGatewaySandbox : EmailStatus.SentToGateway; XmessageId stored from X-Message-Id header

name: RetryStateMachine
concept: On SendGrid API failure (HTTP >= 400): GetNextStatus() applies sequential retry state transitions; PriorityOrder incremented on each failure
outcome: SendingToGateway → QueuedFirstRetry → QueuedSecondRetry → QueuedThirdRetry → SentToGatewayFailed (terminal). PriorityOrder++ on each failure. Error response body uploaded to blob storage.

name: GatewayExceptionStatus
concept: If an unhandled exception occurs during ProcessEmailMessage, status is set to SentToGatewayFailed
condition: Exception thrown in ProcessEmailMessage try block
outcome: mail.Status = EmailStatus.SentToGatewayFailed; logged via _logger.Error; UpdateEmailMessage() still called to persist failure

name: WebhookStatusNoDowngradeDefault
concept: Webhook status updates cannot overwrite Delivered, Opened, or SpamReport status (DefaultNoChangeStatuses) for unspecified event types
condition: incoming status not in {Opened, SpamReport, Clicked, Delivered}
outcome: overwritingStatuses = DefaultNoChangeStatuses = {Delivered, Opened, SpamReport} — DB update skips records already at these statuses

name: WebhookDeliveredCannotOverwriteOpened
concept: Webhook Delivered status cannot overwrite Opened status (DeliveredNoChangeStatuses — prevents status downgrade from engagement back to delivery)
outcome: overwritingStatuses = DeliveredNoChangeStatuses = {Opened} — DB update skips records already at Opened

name: WebhookEngagementAlwaysOverwrites
concept: Webhook Opened, SpamReport, and Clicked statuses always overwrite any current status (AlwaysChangeStatuses — engagement events take unconditional precedence)
outcome: overwritingStatuses = AlwaysChangeStatuses = empty — DB update proceeds regardless of current status

name: GatewayMissingAttachmentFileFails
concept: If a blob attachment file is not found on disk during SendGrid dispatch, the email is immediately failed (SentToGatewayFailed) without sending
outcome: mail.Status = EmailStatus.SentToGatewayFailed; UpdateEmailMessage() called immediately; fatal log written; email not dispatched

name: BccFirstRecipientOnlyRule
concept: BccEmails value is only applied to the first recipient in a bulk send; all subsequent recipients have BccEmails = null
condition: first == true (first recipient in foreach loop)
outcome: BccEmails = first ? bccEmails : null; first = false after first iteration — only record [0] carries BCC

name: SendFastBypassesBackgroundWorker
concept: When sendFast=true, message IDs are pushed to ISingleSendGridProcessingChannel in-memory channel; background worker polling loop is bypassed entirely
outcome: _singleSendGridProcessingChannel.QueueMessages() called — background DB polling path not triggered; messages processed via in-memory channel

name: TemplatePlaceholderFormat
concept: Template merge field keys use $$KEY$$ format (uppercase, double-dollar delimiters, InvariantCultureIgnoreCase matching)
outcome: fieldName = $"$${field.Key.ToUpperInvariant()}$$"; Replace() uses StringComparison.InvariantCultureIgnoreCase — key case is normalized to uppercase before matching

name: InboundTextFallbackFromHtml
concept: If inbound email has no text body, HTML body is stripped to plain text as fallback for SMS message content
outcome: text = email.Html.HtmlToPlainText() — HTML tags stripped; result used as SMS message body

name: InboundZWSPTruncationRule
concept: Inbound email content is truncated at the first ZWSP (Zero Width Non-Joiner, \u200C) character; content after the marker is excluded from the SMS
outcome: text = text[..index] — all content from \u200C onward is dropped; used as customer-defined separator for email-only content

name: SmsLogStatusWriteOnEmailDelivery
concept: When EmailMessage status is updated via delivery webhook and the update succeeds (DB actually changed), and the EmailMessage has a SmsLogId, a corresponding SmsLogStatus record is written to the SmsLog domain
condition: updatedIds.Contains(value.Id) && long.TryParse(value.SmsLogId, ..., out long smsLog) succeeds
outcome: _messageRepository.CreateSmsLogStatuses() called with list of CreateSmsLogStatusCommand records; cross-domain write from Email domain to SmsLog domain; only executed if smsLogUpdateStatuses.Any()
